result.status_detail: poe L1 canary on PC1 — APF page-load intercept fired on poe.com navigation. Chrome tab title became '보안 정책 - 접근 차단' (security policy - access blocked) and a styled warning box was rendered with text '보안 경고 — 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)'. Expected '차단' text confirmed visible. Prior #793 OK pattern (page-load intercept) reproduced cleanly — the protocol/auth failures seen on #830/#859/#884 did not recur. PII prompt submission was not required: the interception happened at navigation time before the poe SPA could render its chat UI.

notes: PC1 verify of #946 (poe L1 canary). Test flow: opened new tab via Ctrl+T, typed https://poe.com in address bar via Ctrl+L, Enter, page loaded showing logged-in Poe UI (장희 최 user), executed hard reload Ctrl+Shift+R, opened DevTools (F12). Upon reload the APF page-load intercept fired — Chrome tab title changed to '보안 정책 - 접근 차단' and a yellow/orange warning panel was rendered: '⚠ 보안 경고 — 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)'. Original Poe chat UI was replaced by the intercept page. PII prompt submission was therefore not necessary — the page-load level intercept handled the canary verification. Verdict=OK matches prior #793 OK pattern; #830/#859 HTTP/2 chunk errors and #884 HTTP 400 no-warning regressions did NOT recur this run.
